violations.text: The non-food contact surfaces of the areas underneath the frying area have an accumulation of grease, food residue, and other debris. The non-food contact surfaces shall be cleaned as often as necessary to prevent buildup of residue. New Violation. Correct By: 04/22/2026

Some droppings observed in the back prep room and in the drive-thru area by the walls. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. -Staff cleaned and swept the areas during this inspection. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/22/2026

The door to the outside, located in the front entrance, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 03/17/2026

The floors in the dry storage area has an accumulation of debris and old rodent droppings. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 03/03/2026

The floors primarily in the mop sink and walk-in cooler areas were observed in poor repair (grout missing). The physical facilities must be maitained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 11/29/2024

Cheese and sauce was observed held at 53-56 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Food was that was stored in unit over night was voluntarily disposed of. Food that was placed in unit at 9:30 this morning was moved to walk-in to rapidly cool. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/15/2023

The reach in unit was observed iced over and was observed not able to hold an adequate ambient temperature. Unit was taken out of service and was getting repaired at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 08/15/2023

The floor in the dry storage area was observed with an accumulation of food and other debris. The hood above the chicken breading station was observed with debris. New Violation. Correct By: 08/15/2023
